theme: multi-head plotter painting a mural without vibration
title: Gantry Heads on a Mural Rail
strategies: trivial: one head does every stroke alone in the given (position) order, swapping whenever the color changes -- exactly the checker's own baseline

greedy (color-setup-batching only): hand each color to one head round robin, sweep that head's strokes color-block by color-block -- minimizes swap count but ignores proximity, so on interleaved-color instances every head sweeps the whole rail and stays mutually throttled almost the entire run

strong (insight): partition the rail into H contiguous space-time territories assigned to the nearest head (keeps heads > D apart, throttling collapses to ~0), then INSIDE each territory group strokes into color runs ordered by mean position -- trades a little extra local backtracking for far fewer swaps, without ever paying the throttle penalty

beyond strong: search over territory boundaries / assignment instead of fixed equal splits, or locally interleave two adjacent color runs when their spans overlap enough that a merged sweep is cheaper than two separate runs
